_meta.description: Per-product privacy and security data for camera-equipped products. Single source of truth for the ProductPrivacyDetails UI on product cards. Updated when manufacturer pages, privacy policies, or independent reviews change.
rules: fromManufacturer values must be directly stated on the manufacturer's product page or privacy policy. If not explicitly stated, use 'not-specified'.

fromThirdParty findings must come from Tier 1 (Mozilla Privacy Not Included, Consumer Reports, FTC) or Tier 2 (established tech/security publications: Ars Technica, Wired, Wirecutter, Tom's Guide, Krebs on Security, How-To Geek, security conferences). Each finding must include source name, URL, and date.
summary is a neutral one-line restatement of what the manufacturer documents — no judgment words.
lastResearched is the date the data was last verified against current manufacturer pages.

valueGlossary.yes: Manufacturer explicitly states the feature is present
valueGlossary.no: Manufacturer explicitly states the feature is absent
valueGlossary.not-specified: Manufacturer does not state either way on their product page or privacy policy
valueGlossary.local-only: Data processed and stored on-device, not transmitted to manufacturer cloud
valueGlossary.local-first: Most data processed/stored on-device, with optional cloud features
valueGlossary.cloud-first: Data transmitted to manufacturer cloud by default
valueGlossary.hybrid: Mix of local and cloud processing depending on feature
valueGlossary.none-disclosed: Manufacturer has not disclosed any security incidents on their product page or privacy policy

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer does not document a physical lens cover on the product page or in the privacy policy.

softwarePrivacyMode.note: The product page does not document a one-tap camera/microphone disable feature (this feature is documented for the EBO Air 2 Plus and EBO Air 2S, but not the EBO Air 2 specifically).

storageLocation.note: Manufacturer states the EBO Air 2 ships with a 32GB microSD card (pre-installed), expandable to 256GB, where videos are stored locally.

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: Privacy policy is published at mediakit.enabot.com. It states Wi-Fi credentials are not saved on local or cloud terminals, and that camera notification data is transmitted via cloud but not stored there.

ebo-air-2-familybot.summary: Manufacturer documents 32GB onboard storage (expandable to 256GB) for video and a privacy policy disclosing Wi-Fi credentials are not saved on local or cloud terminals.

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer does not document a physical lens cover.

softwarePrivacyMode.note: Manufacturer product page describes the EBO Air 2S as a budget-friendly version of the EBO Air 2 Plus but does not directly document a one-tap camera disable for the 2S.

storageLocation.note: Manufacturer states: 'Videos can be stored on an SD card, so no subscription is required for local storage. We also offer an optional subscription for unlimited cloud storage.'

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: Privacy policy is published at mediakit.enabot.com.

ebo-air-2s-familybot.summary: Manufacturer documents local SD card storage as the default with optional cloud storage subscription.

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer does not document a physical lens cover.

softwarePrivacyMode.note: Manufacturer states: 'Instantly disable camera or microphone with a single tap.' Product page also documents user-access management: 'Unified management of access rights and time restrictions.'

storageLocation.note: Manufacturer states: '32GB Built-in (21GB Available) + 32GB microSD Card Included.' Privacy policy adds that face data 'will be saved on the robot locally rather than on the cloud.'

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: EBO HOME ROBOT service agreement states: 'EBO HOME ROBOT will not use your audio, video, picture, and other data saved on the cloud for other [purposes] unless agreed expressly by you or prescribed by compulsory provisions of laws and regulations.'

ebo-air-2-plus-familybot.summary: Manufacturer documents one-tap camera and microphone disable, local face-data processing, 32GB built-in storage plus 32GB microSD, and a service agreement limiting use of cloud-stored audio, video, and pictures.

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer does not document a physical lens cover.

softwarePrivacyMode.note: EBO HOME Robot service agreement and privacy policy apply, but a specific one-tap camera disable is not directly documented for the EBO X.

storageLocation.note: Privacy policy states face data is 'saved on the robot locally rather than on the cloud,' and Wi-Fi credentials 'will not be saved on local or cloud terminal.' Camera notification data 'will only be transmitted on the cloud but not saved there.' Other video and audio may be saved on the cloud per the service agreement.

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: EBO HOME ROBOT service agreement states the manufacturer will not use cloud-stored audio, video, and picture data for other purposes without express consent.

ebo-x-familybot.summary: Manufacturer documents local processing of face data, cloud-only transit (not storage) for camera notifications, and a service agreement limiting use of cloud-stored audio, video, and pictures.

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer does not document a physical lens cover.

storageLocation.note: ROLA Mini is part of Enabot's EBO product family which uses local SD card storage. Specific storage capacity for ROLA Mini is documented as supporting microSD storage on the manufacturer's listings.

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: Privacy policy is published at mediakit.enabot.com. States Wi-Fi credentials are not saved on local or cloud terminals.

fromThirdParty.finding: An earlier device in the EBO line (the EBO SE) was noted to lack a physical power button.
fromThirdParty.note: This review is of an older Enabot model. Whether the ROLA Mini ships with a physical power button is not documented on the current product page.

rola-mini-pet-monitor.summary: Manufacturer documents local microSD card storage and a privacy policy stating Wi-Fi credentials are not saved on local or cloud terminals.

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer describes 'a near-invisible camera nestled in its nose' but does not document a physical lens cover.

softwarePrivacyMode.note: Manufacturer states the 'Dream Sketch' photo feature 'must be manually enabled in the app. The feature is completely optional and will only work if the user chooses to turn it on.' Daily interactions are described as using a local offline AI model with no data uploaded.

storageLocation.note: Manufacturer states: 'During normal interactions with Ropet, it continues to use a local offline AI model, meaning it does not depend on cloud servers and no data is uploaded during everyday use.' When photos are taken via the Dream Sketch feature, the manufacturer states: 'Photos are encrypted before leaving the device... Only the encrypted data is then uploaded to the cloud AI model.'

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: Manufacturer publishes a privacy statement on the product page describing the Dream Sketch data flow and the local-first model for daily interactions.

kamomo.summary: Manufacturer documents a local offline AI model for daily interactions with no data uploaded, an opt-in cloud photo feature with on-device encryption before upload, and a near-invisible camera in the nose without a documented physical shutter.

physicalShutter.note: Manufacturer does not document a physical lens cover.

softwarePrivacyMode.note: Manufacturer product page does not explicitly document a one-tap camera-disable feature.

storageLocation.note: Manufacturer states: 'Loona has a powerful CPU, except for voice recognition, all other robot perception: including face data, human body data, and all other perception data are only processed locally in Loona, not uploaded to any cloud.' Voice is processed via AWS Lex cloud service with 'no user data stored in the cloud or locally, only real-time analysis is performed.' User account information and app settings are stored on AWS servers in the U.S. and Europe.

privacyPolicyAvailable.note: Privacy policy is published at loonaweb.keyirobot.cn and a separate data-privacy support article is published at keyitech.zendesk.com. Policy lists supported security standards: PCI-DSS, HIPAA/HITECH, FedRAMP, GDPR, FIPS 140-2, and NIST 800-171.

robot-pet-dog.summary: Manufacturer documents local-only processing of face and body perception data, AWS Lex voice processing with no data stored, regional AWS server storage for account data, and an encryption chip for remote-monitoring communication.
